slice: Phase 4 slice D — lifecycle/config fixes; see maven-resolution-plan/phase4-results-d.md
harness: maven-resolution-plan/phase0-tools IntegratedBenchmark: MavenParser end-to-end, one JVM, ctx engine=legacy vs engine=maven over identical reactor inputs; warm iterations zero-download (RecordingHttpSender replay).

fixes.D-1: resolver named-lock factory file-lock -> rwlock-local on both session templates (MavenEngine.newSession, EngineDependencyCollector.newSession): eliminates the per-artifact lrm/.locks/*.lock FileChannel that was the FD leak AND the dominant collect cost.
fixes.D-2: shared singleton DefaultModelBuilder (EngineModelBuilderFactory.shared()): super-POM read once, not per model build; removes the JarURLConnection/ZipFileInputStream churn.
fixes.D-3: descriptor-read model cache shared across the collect via session.getCache(); servedBy preserved by collector-level accumulation; root build keeps a fresh store.
fixes.D-4: CacheBridge.resolvePom returns a byte-backed StringModelSource; dropped materialize/materializeDir.
fixes.D-5: ctx-wide cache of BomGavAttributor effective-management keys, keyed by (reactorEpoch, bomGav): collapses the superlinear O(modules x BOMs) BOM rebuild in the root effective phase (camel-400 effectiveMs 8915 -> 3049, modelBuilds 6831 -> 3615).

environment.heap: all tiers at -Xmx2g (the D-1 FD fix makes 2g viable for BOM-heavy reactors that crashed at 2g before)

protocol.warm: fresh MavenPomCache per iteration, zero network (replay); median
protocol.loopCtx: warm-cache re-resolution reusing ONE ExecutionContext (engine + DataPool warm) - the true UpdateMavenModel steady state
protocol.note: legacy and maven within a tier are one JVM run (same-load ratio)

fdFix.rootCause: resolver default named-lock factory is file-based: one FileChannel/FileLock on lrm/.locks/artifact~g~a~pom~v.lock per artifact during the verbose collect; concurrent BOM-heavy reactors hold thousands, and fresh-ctx parses build never-closed RepositorySystems, accumulating to the 92160 per-process cap.
fdFix.evidence: lsof at crash (via -Dbench.fdwatch histogram) showed the open set was almost entirely lrm/.locks/*.lock channels.
fdFix.result: camel-50 at 2g crashed before; now completes with fdBefore=61 -> fdAfter=60 (stable). FD stable at every tier.

tiers.heapNote: maven peakHeapMb is reserved-not-live on a ~370ms/2g run; not a meaningful allocation ratio at this scale

tiers.note: before D-5 the BomGav rebuild made this tier warm 20544ms (3.38x) with GC-thrash variance [20544,16060,27555]; D-5 dropped it to 12042ms with tight variance.
